titleEn: MilkingMilk in the tank with less effort
titleZh: 更省力地实现“奶在罐中”——Lely自动挤奶系统解决方案全景解析
summaryZh: 本文全面介绍了Lely自动挤奶系统的核心理念与产品线，强调通过自由牛流、个体化处理、健康优化和精细奶品管理，实现“更省力地让牛奶入罐”。文章涵盖Astronaut A5 Next、Astronaut Max及Dairy XL等产品，并附有关于选择性干奶牛治疗、机器人放牧融合及挤奶机器人演进的技术文章。内容体现了智慧畜牧中IoT、机器人、传感器与数据驱动的深度融合，为大型牧场提供了可操作的自动化升级路径。
translationZh:
解决方案概览

更省力地实现“奶在罐中”

自由牛流

探索我们的挤奶机器人

探索

**引言**

控制输入，优化产出

您的牧场是一个独特的生态系统，您比任何人都更了解其复杂性。您的奶牛也是如此——它们的习性、偏好和个体细微差别对您而言如同本能。这正是您可以从自动挤奶系统中获得的：它不仅关乎效率，更在于映射您的直觉和行动。

控制输入，优化产出

奶牛的自由选择权

为奶牛提供无压力的环境，保持平和与安静，让它们能够表达自然行为。

个体化处理

每头奶牛，甚至它的每个乳头都是独特的，因此需要个体化对待。

优化奶牛健康

奶牛健康时，生产性能才能达到最优。必须满足健康要求。

精心处理牛奶

牛奶是宝贵但易腐的商品，需要精心处理以保持品质。

挤奶产品概览

自动挤奶机器人

Lely Astronaut A5 Next

适用于大型牧场的自动挤奶解决方案

Astronaut Max

大型牧场

Dairy XL

视频

自由牛流如何运作

通过自由牛流，奶牛自行决定何时进食、饮水、躺卧和挤奶。这改善了它们的福祉。通过照顾奶牛并在奶牛舒适度上投入额外努力，我们能够延长使用寿命并提高产奶量。

文章

养殖洞察

挤奶 2026年5月28日

Lely MQC-C如何支持选择性干奶牛治疗

干奶期对奶牛的健康和生产力起着关键作用。它让乳房从前一个泌乳期恢复，并为奶牛成功开始下一个泌乳期做好准备。同时，这也是降低早期泌乳期乳腺炎和乳房感染风险的关键时刻。

近年来，奶牛养殖已转向更具针对性的方法：选择性干奶牛治疗。不再对所有奶牛在干奶时使用抗生素，而是根据个体奶牛健康状况做出治疗决策。准确及时的体细胞数（SCC）洞察对此方法至关重要。

挤奶 2026年4月7日

使用挤奶机器人放牧：挑战还是机遇？

放牧在许多地区的奶牛养殖中扮演重要角色，尤其是春季奶牛返回牧场时。同时，许多牧场已采用自动挤奶系统。对于使用机器人的牧场主来说，将放牧与挤奶机器人结合常常引发一个根本性问题：放牧是成为挑战，还是可以转化为机遇？

在机器人挤奶系统中，奶牛自行决定何时挤奶，全天遵循自己的节奏。引入放牧会改变奶牛的动机和活动，从而影响它们访问机器人的频率。这就是为什么放牧和机器人挤奶常被视为难以结合的原因。

在实践中，在适当条件下，放牧可以与挤奶机器人良好配合。理解奶牛如何平衡进食、休息和挤奶，以及放牧如何影响这种平衡，是维持产奶量、奶牛健康和牧场运营顺畅的关键。

挤奶 2026年4月1日

机器人挤奶的演进

三十多年来，机器人挤奶改变了奶牛牧场的运营方式。最初作为减少体力劳动的想法，已发展成一个支持牧场主、奶牛和长期牧场绩效的智能系统。

早期的机器人系统旨在解决一个关键挑战：如何在无需直接人工干预的情况下安全、一致地挤奶。随着时间的推移，这些系统发展成为可靠的数据驱动工具，支持自由牛流、灵活的工作时间和更健康的牛群。

如今，机器人挤奶不仅关乎效率。它关乎创造一个让奶牛能够遵循自然节奏的牛舍环境，让牧场主从数据中获得洞察，让日常工作更加可持续和面向未来。

挤奶 2026年4月1日

自动挤奶机器人的持续发展

自动挤奶技术已改变了奶牛养殖，但Lely的旅程远未结束。自动挤奶系统的持续发展是我们创新战略的核心。Lely Astronaut年复一年地不断演进，由新知识、新技术和牧场上的日常挑战驱动。从外观上看，Lely Astronaut可能相似，但内部每年都在进行改进，以保持我们的解决方案可持续、盈利且令人愉悦。这证明了机器人挤奶不是一个固定产品，而是一个持续发展的解决方案，旨在支持今天的牧场主和未来的牧场。

contentFull:
Solutions overview

Milking solutions

Milk in the tank with less effort

Free cow traffic

Optimise cow health

Individual treatment

Discover our milking robot

Explore

INTRODUCTION

Control the input, optimize the output

Your farm is a unique ecosystem, and you understand its intricacies better than anyone else. The same goes for your cows – their habits, preferences, and individual nuances are second nature to you. That’s precisely what you can expect from an automatic milking system: it’s not only about efficiency; it’s about mirroring your instincts and actions. 

Control the input, optimize the output

Cow freedom of choice

Provide the cows with a stress-free environment with peace and quiet that allows hem to express their natural behaviour.

Individual treatment

Every cow and even every one of her teats is unique and, therefore, needs to be treated individually. 

Optimise cow health

Cow performance is optimised when cows are healthy. Health requirements must be met.

Handle milk with care

Milk is a valuable but perishable commodity and needs to be handled with care to maintain the quality.

Milking products overview

Automatic milking robot

Lely Astronaut A5 Next

Automatic milking solution for large dairy farms

Astronaut Max

Large farming

Dairy XL

Video

How free cow traffic works

With free cow traffic, the cow decides when she eats, drinks, lies down and is milked. This improves her wellbeing. By taking care of our cows and putting extra effort into cow comfort, we are able to extend lifetime and milk production.

Articles

Farming insights

Milking5/28/2026

How the Lely MQC-C supports selective dry cow therapy

The dry period plays a crucial role in the health and productivity of dairy cows. It allows the udder to recover from the previous lactation and prepares the cow for a successful start of the next one. At the same time, it is a key moment to reduce the risk of mastitis and udder infection in early lactation.

In recent years, dairy farming has moved towards a more targeted approach: selective dry cow therapy. Instead of treating all cows with antibiotics at dry off, treatment decisions are based on individual cow health. Accurate and timely insight into somatic cell count (SCC) is essential for this approach.

Milking4/7/2026

Grazing with a milking robot: challenge or opportunity?

Grazing plays an important role in dairy farming in many regions, especially when cows return to pasture in spring. At the same time, many farms already work with automatic milking systems. For robot farmers, combining grazing with a milking robot often raises a fundamental question: does grazing become a challenge, or can it turn into an opportunity?

In a robotic milking system, cows decide for themselves when to be milked, following their own rhythm throughout the day. Introducing grazing changes cow motivation and movement, which can influence how often cows visit the robot. This is why grazing and robotic milking are often seen as a difficult combination.

In practice, grazing can work well with a milking robot under the right conditions. Understanding how cows balance eating, resting and milking, and how grazing influences that balance, is key to maintaining milk production, cow health and smooth farm operations during the grazing season.

Milking4/1/2026

The evolution of robotic milking

For more than three decades, robotic milking has changed the way dairy farms operate. What began as an idea to reduce physical labour has grown into a smart system that supports farmers, cows, and long-term farm performance.

Early robotic systems were designed to solve one key challenge: how to milk cows safely and consistently without direct human involvement. Over time, these systems developed into reliable, data-driven tools that support free cow traffic, flexible working hours, and healthier herds.

Today, robotic milking is not only about efficiency. It is about creating a barn environment where cows can follow their natural rhythm, farmers gain insight from the data, and daily work becomes more sustainable and future-proof.

Milking4/1/2026

Continuous development of the automatic milking robot

Automatic milking technology has transformed dairy farming, but the journey is far from finished at Lely. The continuous development of the automatic milking system is at the heart of our innovation strategy. The Lely Astronaut continues to evolve year after year, driven by new knowledge, new technology and the daily challenges on farms. On the outside, the Lely Astronaut may look similar, but on the inside improvements are made every single year to keep our solution sustainable, profitable and enjoyable to work with. This proves that robotic milking is not a fixed product, but a continuously developing solution built to support farmers today and in the future.
